title: Decision Receipt v0.1.1
description: Constitutional decision artifact. Records what was decided, under what authority, with what evidence, and what disposition followed. v0.1.1 adds optional decision_basis for structured evidence context.

receipt_id.description: Unique identifier for this receipt. UUID v4.

receipt_version.description: Schema version. Consumers MUST reject unknown versions.

ceid.description: Constitutional Entity ID if available. Null during migration or if CEID generation fails.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when the decision was made.

parent_receipt_id.description: Receipt ID of the prior decision this one supersedes or continues. Null for first-in-chain.

supersedes.description: Receipt ID of a decision this one explicitly reverses or replaces. Null if original.

decision_type.description: What kind of constitutional decision this is. Free string, not enum-locked. Recommended v0 values: gate_evaluation, proof_tier_determination, admissibility_verdict, refusal_classification, settlement_transition, escalation_determination, obligation_assessment, contradiction_resolution, rollback_authorization. New types do not require schema revision.

decision_subject.description: Stable identifier of what this decision is about. E.g. tool grant request ID, settlement packet ID, claim ID, transition ID, proof-tier candidate ID. Enables joining decisions to their subjects without parsing verdict_reason.

verdict.description: The decision outcome. Six canonical values.

verdict_reason.description: Human-readable explanation of why this verdict was reached. Not machine-parsed. Max 500 chars.

verdict_reason_codes.description: Machine-readable reason codes for the verdict. Ordered by significance.

authority_class.description: CCOI authority class under which this decision was made.

authority_scope.description: What jurisdiction this authority covers (e.g. 'tool_visibility', 'proof_tier', 'provider_routing', 'settlement'). Prevents authority cross-contamination.

delegated_from.description: If this decision was made under delegated authority, the ID of the delegating authority. Null for primary authority.

policy_id.description: Identifier of the policy that governed this decision.

policy_hash.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the JCS-canonicalized effective policy bundle applied at decision time, including inherited rules after resolution. Computed as: sha256(jcs_canonicalize(resolved_policy_document)). Prevents silent policy swap between decision and audit.

policy_hash_alg.description: Hash algorithm used for policy_hash. Always 'sha256' in v0.1.

policy_version.description: Human-readable policy version if available. Recommended format: '<policy_name>.v<N>' (e.g. 'guardian.capability_gate.v3').

episode_id.description: Episode within which this decision was made.

session_state_hash.description: SHA-256 prefix (16 hex chars) of the canonical session state at decision time. Enables replay verification.

proof_tier_at_decision.description: Proof tier of the episode when this decision was made.

runtime_condition_vector.description: RCV snapshot at decision time. Observational only. Null if no signal was available.

evidence_refs.description: References to evidence considered for this decision. Each ref is a typed pointer, not the evidence itself.

ref_id.description: Identifier of the referenced evidence artifact.

ref_uri.description: Locator for the evidence artifact. URI, path, or system-qualified reference. Null if ref_id is sufficient for resolution within the same system.

ref_hash.description: SHA-256 of the referenced artifact for integrity binding.

ref_role.description: What role this evidence played in the decision.

evidence_sufficient.description: Whether the decider judged evidence to be sufficient for this verdict under the governing policy and proof tier. Sufficient means: the evidence meets the minimum threshold defined by the active policy for a verdict of this type. False means: evidence was below threshold, which constrains the verdict (APPROVE is forbidden when false).

evidence_gaps.description: Named gaps in evidence that were identified but not resolved at decision time.

decision_basis.description: Structured record of what information was considered, excluded, and missing at decision time. Optional. When present, provides human-reviewable transparency into the evidentiary foundation of the decision. Added in v0.1.1.

admitted.description: Information that was available and used in reaching the decision.

claim.description: Concise statement of what was considered. Must be human-reviewable.

source.description: Where this information came from (system, tool, user, policy, etc.).

freshness.description: How current the information was at decision time.

role.description: What role this information played in the decision.

excluded.description: Information that was available but deliberately not used, with reasons.

claim.description: Concise statement of what was excluded.

source.description: Where the excluded information came from.

gaps.description: Information that was expected but not available at decision time.

impact.description: How the absence affected the decision.

searched.description: Sources that were checked for this information.

confidence.description: Decider's epistemic confidence in the verdict. Enum band, not a float — bands are honest about the granularity of self-assessment. This is an epistemic summary chosen by the authority, constrained by evidence sufficiency and proof tier; it is not a probabilistic claim unless a policy explicitly defines it as such. Null means confidence was not assessed.

conflict_refs.description: Receipt IDs of authoritative decisions that are incompatible with this one. Used when verdict=CONFLICT. Distinct from dissent: dissent is advisory disagreement metadata; conflict_refs are authoritative contradictions requiring escalation.

dissent.description: Structured dissent record if any advisory input disagreed with the verdict. Null if no dissent.

dissenter_ids.description: Identifiers of advisory sources that disagreed.

dissent_summary.description: Human-readable summary of the dissenting position. Max 300 chars.

abstention_reason.description: If verdict is ABSTAIN, why the authority declined to decide. Null otherwise.

unresolved_contradictions.description: Contradictions that existed at decision time and were NOT resolved by this decision.

disposition.description: What action followed from this decision.

disposition_target.description: If disposition routes to a specific system or actor, its identifier. E.g. 'human_review', 'guardian_override', 'loom:spine:controller'.

obligations_created.description: New obligations that this decision created (must be satisfied before next transition).

proof_tier_achieved.description: If this decision determined or changed a proof tier, the tier achieved. Null if decision does not affect proof tier.

proof_tier_minimum_required.description: Minimum proof tier required by policy for this decision to be valid. Null if no minimum was specified.

provenance_complete.description: Whether all required provenance fields for claimed support are present and resolvable. True means: every evidence_ref has a valid ref_id, policy_hash matches a retrievable policy, and no known_provenance_gaps exist. False means: the receipt is structurally valid but epistemically degraded — some upstream references cannot be verified.

known_provenance_gaps.description: Fields that should be populated but are not, with reasons.

content_hash.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the JCS-canonicalized receipt with signature and content_hash fields removed. Enables content-addressable lookup.

signature.description: Ed25519 signature over the JCS-canonicalized receipt with signature field removed. Null if unsigned.

signer_pubkey_sha256.description: SHA-256 fingerprint of the signing public key. Null if unsigned.
